phase1Findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
phase1Findings.description: Use of a weak or broken cryptographic algorithm detected. Use modern alternatives (e.g., SHA-256, AES-256).

phase1Findings.title: Potential SQL Injection
phase1Findings.description: User input appears to be concatenated directly into a SQL query. Use parameterized queries instead.

location.snippet: f"UPDATE supplier_orders SET status=?, {ts_field}=? WHERE id=?",

phase1Findings.title: Potential Command Injection
phase1Findings.description: User input may be passed to a shell command. Use safe APIs that avoid shell interpretation.

location.snippet: result = subprocess.run([HYPERSPACE_CLI] + args, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=30)

phase1Findings.title: JWT None Algorithm Vulnerability
phase1Findings.description: JWT verification without algorithm restriction can allow forged tokens. Always specify allowed algorithms.

confirmedVulnerabilities.title: LLM Output Execution: eval/exec on AI response
confirmedVulnerabilities.description: LLM output is passed to eval() or exec(). An attacker who controls the prompt can achieve arbitrary code execution.

location.snippet: result = subprocess.run([HYPERSPACE_CLI] + args, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=30)

confirmedVulnerabilities.title: API: No Pagination on List Endpoint
confirmedVulnerabilities.description: Database query returns all records without limit. Enables data dumping and denial of service.

confirmedVulnerabilities.title: API: CORS Allows All Origins
confirmedVulnerabilities.description: CORS configured with wildcard or 'true' origin. Any website can make authenticated requests to your API.

confirmedVulnerabilities.title: API: CORS Allows All Origins
confirmedVulnerabilities.description: CORS configured with wildcard or 'true' origin. Any website can make authenticated requests to your API.

confirmedVulnerabilities.title: Headers: Missing Content-Security-Policy
confirmedVulnerabilities.description: No Content-Security-Policy header found. CSP prevents XSS, clickjacking, and data injection attacks.

location.snippet: No Content-Security-Policy found in server files

confirmedVulnerabilities.title: Headers: Missing Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS)
confirmedVulnerabilities.description: No HSTS header. Browsers may connect via HTTP, enabling man-in-the-middle attacks.

location.snippet: No Strict-Transport-Security found in server files

confirmedVulnerabilities.title: Headers: Missing X-Frame-Options
confirmedVulnerabilities.description: No X-Frame-Options header. Pages can be embedded in iframes, enabling clickjacking attacks.

location.snippet: No X-Frame-Options found in server files

confirmedVulnerabilities.title: Headers: Missing X-Content-Type-Options
confirmedVulnerabilities.description: No X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff header. Browsers may MIME-sniff responses, enabling XSS via file uploads.

location.snippet: No X-Content-Type-Options found in server files

confirmedVulnerabilities.title: Headers: Missing Referrer-Policy
confirmedVulnerabilities.description: No Referrer-Policy header. Sensitive URL parameters may leak to external sites via the Referer header.

location.snippet: No Referrer-Policy found in server files

confirmedVulnerabilities.title: Headers: Missing Permissions-Policy
confirmedVulnerabilities.description: No Permissions-Policy header. Third-party scripts can access camera, microphone, and geolocation.

location.snippet: No Permissions-Policy|Feature-Policy found in server files

confirmedVulnerabilities.title: Race Condition: TOCTOU in File Operations
confirmedVulnerabilities.description: File existence checked before use. Between check and use, the file could be modified or removed by another process.

confirmedVulnerabilities.title: Race Condition: Non-Atomic Read-Modify-Write
confirmedVulnerabilities.description: Value read from database/storage, modified, then written back without locking. Concurrent requests can overwrite each other's changes.

confirmedVulnerabilities.title: Race Condition: No Idempotency Check
confirmedVulnerabilities.description: Payment or state-changing operation without idempotency key or duplicate check. Concurrent requests can process the same operation twice.
